behavior_summary: U8P_METEOR::hit is a process that runs when the meteor is struck: it plays a U8P_METEOR::hit sound, optionally forwards a randomized U8P_METEOR::hit to a target/selector passed in via its first parameter, and then destroys the meteor object.

If the first parameter (param1) is a valid selector value (inferred check: > 1 and < 256), the function copies that value to a local (local1) and calls a receive-U8P_METEOR::hit helper to apply randomized damage/effect to that selector.

Randomization is applied to the forwarded U8P_METEOR::hit using two urandom calls (one added to a base 0x0A yielding an inferred primary damage ~10–29, and a smaller secondary random 0–7), then Item::destroy(this) removes the meteor instance.
notable_control_flow: Always: playSFX(0x64, 0x1F) is invoked at function start to produce immediate audio feedback.
Conditional branch: if param1 is in the valid selector range (inferred >1 and <256) then prepare local1 and call METHOD::receiveHitWrapper with randomized parameters.
Termination/cleanup: Item::destroy(this) is called unconditionally (or after the conditional) to remove the meteor object.
open_questions: What is the intended semantic meaning of the second parameter (param2)? It is present in the signature but unused in the excerpt.
Exact meanings of METHOD::receiveHitWrapper's arguments (especially the small secondary random value and the 0x00 flag) are inferred but not explicit.
Whether the selector/target is always an entity id when param1 is in range, or whether other selector encodings are supported by the engine.

main_function.name: U8P_METEOR::hit
main_function.purpose: Handle the meteor being U8P_METEOR::hit: play a U8P_METEOR::hit sound, optionally forward a randomized U8P_METEOR::hit to a target/selector passed in, then destroy the meteor object.
entry_conditions: Invoked as the meteor's 'U8P_METEOR::hit' process (process type 0x020B) — triggered when the meteor receives a U8P_METEOR::hit event.
Behavior branches depending on the numeric value of the first parameter (param1).

function_map.name: U8P_METEOR::hit
function_map.role: meteor U8P_METEOR::hit handler (plays SFX, forwards U8P_METEOR::hit, cleans up meteor)

function_map.name: playSFX
function_map.role: play an immediate sound effect for the U8P_METEOR::hit

function_map.name: urandom
function_map.role: produce small random integers used to vary forwarded U8P_METEOR::hit parameters

function_map.name: METHOD::receiveHitWrapper
function_map.role: apply/forward a U8P_METEOR::hit (damage/effect) to the target/selector

external_calls.purpose: Provide immediate audio feedback for the meteor being struck (constants 0x64, 0x1F).
Generate randomized values for the forwarded U8P_METEOR::hit: one urandom(0x14) used as primary damage offset, another urandom(0x08) used as a small secondary parameter.
Forward/apply a U8P_METEOR::hit to the selector/target using randomized parameters and a pointer to local1 (copy of param1). Exact semantics of the parameters are inferred.
Destroy the meteor object instance to clean up after the U8P_METEOR::hit is processed.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_METEOR::hit is a process that runs when the meteor is struck: it plays a U8P_METEOR::hit sound, optionally forwards a randomized U8P_METEOR::hit to a target/selector passed in via its first parameter, and then destroys the meteor object.
